invariants.claim: get_poster RAISES rather than build a DryRunPoster when live
invariants.accurate_statement: The malformed-provider path does not currently create a false published row, but it violates the live-mode fail-closed contract and causes a delayed operational failure.
invariants.root_cause: The guard at post/__init__.py:19 is case-INSENSITIVE and matches only the literal string 'dryrun'. The PROVIDERS lookup at providers.py:56 is case-SENSITIVE with lowercase keys. Any backend string absent from PROVIDERS falls through to `return DryRunPoster(cfg)` at post/__init__.py:29 with no live check.

executed_matrix.Accounts.validate(): clean — NO FLAG for ANY malformed value, including 'blotato'
executed_matrix.effective_provider(): returns the raw malformed string verbatim
executed_matrix.get_media_uploader(): returns the dryrun file:// uploader for every malformed value

invariants.write_boundary: SOUND. set_backend (accounts.py:412) strips + lowercases and rejects unknowns — 'Postiz'/'POSTIZ'/'postiz '/' postiz' all STORE as 'postiz'; 'blotato' raises ValueError. Malformed values are therefore HAND-EDIT ONLY (accounts.json is documented as operator-hand-edited at accounts.py:112).
invariants.validation_gap: Account.backends is dict[str,str] — unvalidated at load (accounts.py:91). Accounts.validate() checks only the integration/backend PAIRING (accounts.py:241-250), never the backend VALUE.

terminal_state.at_least_one_valid_channel_remains: live_ready_channels() is non-empty -> cfg.is_live_backend=True -> the reconcile pass runs (pipeline.py:318) -> post: submitting -> (72h) needs_reconcile (reconcile.py:748) -> (72h) 'GAVE UP:' marker (reconcile.py:759). NO half-live warning fires, because a valid route exists — the operator sees a healthy system while one channel silently publishes nothing. DELAYED OPERATIONAL FAILURE.
terminal_state.all_channels_malformed: live_ready_channels()=[] -> cfg.is_live_backend=False -> the reconcile pass NEVER RUNS (pipeline.py:318) -> the post is stranded in `submitting` PERMANENTLY: no escalation, no error_reason, no terminal state. The half-live banner DOES fire at system level (doctor.py:322, views.py:723), but nothing marks the post. Reachable only if the operator went live with a valid config and hand-edited it broken afterwards (go_live gates on >=1 live-ready channel).

invariants.worst_property: Whitespace variants ('postiz ' / ' postiz') are visually IDENTICAL to the correct value in any UI, JSON dump, or diff.

invariants.claim: 00_control/ledger.lock is vestigial (Cycle-1 DEAD-005)
invariants.executed_race: PROVEN by experiment. A live ledger writer holding BEGIN IMMEDIATE had restore_snapshot os.replace() the database file out from under it. The writer's commit() SUCCEEDED — no exception — and committed to the now-orphaned inode. Its data was SILENTLY DISCARDED. Final on-disk state was the snapshot's, not the writer's.
invariants.why: restore_snapshot takes fcntl.flock on 00_control/ledger.lock (ledger.py:551). EVERY other ledger writer serializes on the SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction (ledger_sqlite.py:92). The two primitives are mutually invisible. restore then calls os.replace on the db path (ledger_sqlite.py:151) and unlinks the -wal/-shm sidecars (ledger_sqlite.py:148-150) while another connection may hold them open.
invariants.exposure: restore_snapshot has NO production caller — it is an operator break-glass classmethod. But ledger_wipe.py:246 advertises it as THE wipe rollback path, so an operator following the documented recovery procedure while the daemon is running hits this.
invariants.reclassification: DEAD-005 was filed 'confirmed dead' on the strength of the word 'vestigial' in a comment. It is a LIVE single-holder lock that provides no mutual exclusion. New classification: live_lock_no_mutual_exclusion.

invariants.claim: The R1 published-URL invariant is bound at the type level so no door can produce the ghost row Post(state=published, public_url='')
invariants.proof: Executed against pydantic 2.13.4. Construction RAISES. model_copy(update=...) does NOT. Direct setattr does NOT (Post has no validate_assignment). The bad row model_dump()s cleanly, and the NEXT Ledger.load raises ValidationError -> ControlFileError (ledger.py:465), making the ENTIRE ledger unloadable.
invariants.false_subclaim: actions.py:281 and cli.py:392 both assert 'Pydantic re-validates the modified instance on serialization'. It does not. The mechanism named in the comments does not exist.
invariants.property_holds_via: Four independent MANUAL call-site guards: run.py:305 (public_url non-empty check), actions.py:268 (mark_published rejects blank url), cli.py:384 (--url required for terminal states), dryrun.py:39 (DryRunPoster sets no state post-M2). track.py:192 is guarded transitively (published->analyzed).
invariants.failure_mode: Not a rejected write — a LOAD-TIME POISON PILL. A 6th door without a manual guard would save successfully and brick the ledger on next process start.

invariants.claim: model_copy is a safe immutable setter (the project's stated immutability rule; used at 57 sites)
invariants.proof: Executed. model_copy bypasses EVERY validator on EVERY model — including Moment's, DESPITE validate_assignment=True. validate_assignment protects setattr ONLY; it has no effect on model_copy.
invariants.why_not_reachable: set_segments (actions_segments.py:19) deliberately round-trips through Moment.model_validate, which DOES run validators, and uses model_copy only for content_token (no validator). clear_segments sets segments=[], where the envelope validator is a no-op. No code path model_copy's Post.account or a non-empty Moment.segments.
invariants.latent_hazard: PROVEN: model_copy(update={'segments': [(2.0,4.0)]}) leaves start=0.0, end=5.0 — a STALE ENVELOPE. The Moment's declared window and its segments disagree, and the clip would render the wrong span.
invariants.coupling: actions_casting.cast_add/cast_remove (actions_casting.py:26,44) are safe ONLY because Moment.validate_assignment=True. Converting them to model_copy 'for consistency' would silently stop canonicalizing affinities — and affinity_admits (the crosspost gate) matches canonical handles, so a non-canonical '@Foo' would make the moment post NOWHERE.

invariants.claim: do NOT set a post's state to queued anywhere except Ledger.approve_post
invariants.property_holds_via: Every non-approve_post writer is guarded on a SOURCE state of failed/error/submitting. None can move a post out of awaiting_approval. Enforced by approve_post's guard at ledger.py:579, not by a single-writer property.

invariants.claim: the intro_match gate is live
invariants.note: Wired into the pipeline and writes real gate files, but absent from responder._SCHEMA/_PROMPT and from gate_keys.gate_source_id. answer_pending iterates _SCHEMA only, so the autonomous responder can NEVER answer it. Moment.intro_matches is never written; no intro-tease plan can be produced.
invariants.dormant_behind_flag: FANOPS_INTRO_TEASE (config.py:731, DEFAULT OFF). With it ON + FANOPS_RESPONDER=llm, unanswerable .request.json files accumulate in 04_agent_io/requests/ forever, invisible to the by-source status view (gate_source_id returns None -> pipeline_status omits them).

invariants.claim: Settings is a typed env boundary constructed per Config()
invariants.note: Config.__init__ never constructs a Settings. All 74 os.getenv calls in config.py read the env directly, uncached, per access. Settings.runtime_load — whose docstring makes the claim — has ZERO callers. Settings' only consumers: doctor (strict validation), config_introspect (docs), accounts (a constant import).
invariants.no_runtime_conflict: There is no precedence to adjudicate, because there is no runtime handoff.
invariants.real_hazard: Two hand-maintained parsers for one env surface; _VALID_BACKENDS defined twice (config.py:72 and settings.py:18); FANOPS_ROOT read by Config but absent from Settings (so invisible to config_introspect).
invariants.divergence_verdict: Intentional strict/lenient split — doctor is fatal, runtime is fail-open. Not drift.

invariants.claim: cascade protection gates deletes on live CLIP states
invariants.note: _LIVE_CLIP_STATES = (published, analyzed). Nothing writes either, so clip_live at ledger.py:665 is ALWAYS False and every branch it gates is unreachable. The protection property holds entirely via _PROTECTED_POST_STATES (ledger.py:667,675), and is sound: when no protected post hangs off a clip, the cascade deletes the posts too (ledger.py:670), so nothing is orphaned. The clip half of the guard is dead defensive code.

invariants.claim: no-auto-publish: an unapproved post is structurally unpublishable
invariants.note: Three mint sites, all born awaiting_approval; the model DEFAULT is awaiting_approval, so even a bare Post(...) is unpublishable. Both publish entry points iterate queued only. Defence-in-depth: a timeless queued post PARKS rather than auto-publishing (run.py:381).

invariants.claim: _publish_one is the SOLE network-POST caller
needs_reconcile is never downgraded to failed
AuthError halts the run, never burns the queue
invariants.note: Matched by TYPE, not substring — the H8 fix.

invariants.claim: ledger writes are atomic (BEGIN IMMEDIATE + full replace)
a newer on-disk schema is refused, never downgraded
bias actuators are amplify-only + validation-frozen
invariants.note: The documented exception is honest and self-declared: variant_ucb is NOT validation-frozen (config.py:821-827).

invariants.claim: _JITTER_MAX < _STEP_MIN => schedule monotonic in index
invariants.note: The ONLY import-time assert in the codebase.

invariants.claim: builtin hash() is banned (PEP 456 per-interpreter salt)
[framing] is the only fail-CLOSED optional dependency
go_live is the ONLY setter of FANOPS_LIVE=1
invariants.note: os.environ[...] is written in exactly 2 places in src/; only golive writes FANOPS_LIVE.

invariants.claim: every ledger model is forward-compatible (pydantic extra=ignore)
CLAUDE.md safety-claim line references
invariants.note: 10 of 10 checked are stale. Function names and semantics are correct; only the citations rotted — which matters because they are how a future editor finds the guard they are told not to break.

invariants.claim: Studio route authorization
invariants.note: 149 routes, 108 mutating, 0 authenticated, 0 CSRF-protected. No before_request hook, no session, no token. Recorded decision: studio/CLAUDE.md says 'no auth by design — don't add CSRF/rate-limit tickets; declined as out-of-scope for localhost'. The boundary is the network interface (default 127.0.0.1:8787). Any page the operator visits while the Studio runs can forge a POST to /golive/live, /schedule/publish-due, or /live-library/wipe/confirm.
invariants.known_gap: MOL-71 (studio/CLAUDE.md): do_wipe_confirm has NO server-side check that do_wipe_preview ran first — 'preview before confirm' is a UI convention only. The typed-word + snapshot gates are unaffected.

invariants.claim: PostState.retired and PostState.error have zero writers (Cycle-2's own initial claim)
invariants.correction: PostState.retired HAS a writer: cli.py:395 (p.state = PostState(args.status)), reachable via `fanops resolve <id> retired` — argparse choices=[published, failed, analyzed, retired] at cli.py:702. PostState.analyzed likewise has TWO writers (track.py:193 AND cli.py:395), not one. PostState.error SURVIVES as reserved (not in the argparse choices; the Studio twin actions.resolve_post explicitly refuses anything but published/failed at actions.py:858).
invariants.root_cause: The original census grepped for LITERAL enum references ('\.state = PostState\.'). It missed generic and dynamic writers: PostState(<string>), p.state = <variable>, setattr(p, <variable>, v), model_copy(update=<variable>), and enum-valued KEYWORD DEFAULTS. An AST census over the full executable tree found five such writers.

invariants.claim: os.environ has no cross-process propagation; a Studio go_live cannot reach a resident daemon (Cycle-2's own COUP-02)
invariants.correction: The daemon loop calls load_dotenv(cfg.root/'.env', override=True) AND rebuilds Config(cfg.root) on EVERY TICK (cli.py:1303-1304, commented 'operator disk truth each tick'). A go_live .env write DOES reach the resident daemon within one tick.
invariants.real_coupling_opposite_direction: A RUNNING Studio calls load_dotenv exactly ONCE, at process entry (cli.py:795), then blocks in app.run (cli.py:1285). So a .env change made by the CLI or the daemon NEVER reaches a running Studio. golive.py:11 acknowledges this outright — it is precisely why _dual_write also pokes os.environ, to make the writing Studio process reflect its own change immediately.
